notes: Strict comparison covers the first local customerSet create branch. The same fixture records id update, email and phone upsert/update, multi-address replacement, empty/null address replacement behavior, duplicate email/phone validation, identifier/input alignment validation, nullable taxExempt validation, deleted-id validation, downstream customer/customerByIdentifier/customers/customersCount visibility, and custom-id no-definition top-level error evidence; those multi-step branches are covered by integration tests because the parity runner currently compares one primary mutation request per scenario. Positive identifier.customId remains unsupported until customer unique-metafield definitions and values are modeled locally.

expectedDifferences.reason: The proxy stages customerSet locally and returns a stable synthetic Customer GID instead of Shopify's live Customer GID.
The proxy stages customerSet with a deterministic local timestamp while Shopify returns the live write timestamp.
The proxy stages customerSet with a deterministic local timestamp while Shopify returns the live write timestamp.
